findings.title: PATH prioritizes user-writable directories for all child process lookups
findings.description: The plist syntax is fine, and `EnvironmentVariables` is a valid launchd key, but this PATH puts `__HOME_DIR__/.local/bin` and `__HOME_DIR__/.cargo/bin` ahead of system locations. In a launchd-managed service, any subprocess started by basename will now resolve through those user-writable directories first, which makes behavior less deterministic and can allow accidental or malicious shadowing of expected tools.
findings.suggestion: Prefer absolute paths for invoked tools (`claude`, `npm`, `cargo`) in the application or wrapper script. If a PATH is still needed, keep it as narrow as possible and avoid putting user-writable directories before trusted system locations unless that precedence is strictly required.

summary: The plist/XML change is structurally valid, but the new PATH is broader than launchd best practice and introduces a user-controlled command resolution risk.
